exceptions: js: OPEN ENGINE BUG (not a flag exception): shared-GC-heap corruption under the pinned GIL-off flag set at W>=4 — GC-dependent under-marking; live cells swept and re-allocated (repro: js/repro-bigint-shared-ingest.js, 0/4 corrupt with --useGC=0, 4/4 with GC on). js W>=4 cells are recorded as failed per SPEC §6; js W>=2 runs with wrong checksums are quarantined as failed instead of aborting the batch. go/java cells and the js W=1 baseline are unaffected.
run1.handicap: **Known structural handicap:** GC under JS threads on this branch is currently stop-the-world with parallel marking; concurrent marking is designed (SPEC-congc.md) but not implemented. Go and Java ship fully concurrent collectors. Allocation-heavy phases (A especially) are expected to show STW pauses scaling with heap size and thread count. Results must be reported with this stated up front, not buried.

run2.handicap: **Known structural handicap:** GC under JS threads on this branch is currently stop-the-world with parallel marking; concurrent marking is designed (SPEC-congc.md) but not implemented. Go and Java ship fully concurrent collectors. Allocation-heavy phases (A especially) are expected to show STW pauses scaling with heap size and thread count. Results must be reported with this stated up front, not buried.

p0_residual.summary: Residual shared-heap corruption crash, DISTINCT from the fixed under-marking bug (25375a997f4f): GC-INDEPENDENT (still fires with --useGC=0, 3/20 at smoke W=4) and gone with --useSharedGCHeap=0 (0/20). Standalone smoke-size rate ~8% (5/61) at W=4; at full N_BASE=28000 kills 1/5 W=2, 3/5 W=4, 3/5 W=8, 5/5 at W>=16. Always loud (SIGTRAP/SIGABRT/SIGSEGV) - zero wrong checksums emitted in 113 successful runs.
signatures: LLInt op_call_varargs -> setupVarargsFrame/JSCellButterfly::copyToArguments on a corrupt arguments array (spread at js/bench.js:216 String.fromCharCode(...codes), thread-local array)
JSObject::toPrimitive/getPropertySlot on a cell with null structure
WTF::StringImpl::deref on a garbage pointer -> libpas 'Large heap did not find object' panic (exit 133)
p0_residual.cores: Tools/threads/scalebench/out/p0-cores/ (core.1986672 varargs-CrashOnOverflow, core.1986681 copyToArguments SEGV, core.2007431 libpas StringImpl deref with --useGC=0, core.2011759 null-structure toPrimitive with --useGC=0)

singlethread_tax_quiet_host.workload: JSTests/threads/scaling/map-heavy.js T(1) scale 1, medians of 5, quiet host (loadavg<1.5)
singlethread_tax_quiet_host.attribution: entirely --useSharedGCHeap=1: pinned-minus-sharedGCHeap 1465ms (= GIL-on level, 3 runs), pinned-minus-sharedAtomTable 2715ms (no change). RSS same attribution: pinned 3.72GB vs minus-sharedGCHeap 155MB vs GIL-on 152MB on identical work.
singlethread_tax_quiet_host.rss_note: big-program js W=1 RSS 11.9GB run2 vs 421MB run1 (28x); wall 33.4s vs 24.3s (+37%) - the under-marking fix correlates with severe shared-heap over-retention/under-collection

parallel_self_giloff.command: SCALING_CELL_TIMEOUT_SECS=1800 Tools/threads/scaling-gate.sh --threads '1 2 4 8' Tools/threads/scalebench/out/jsc-giloff (report mode, medians of 3)
